rationale_en: A single 200-billion-rial bond tranche is negligible against the aggregate stock of government liabilities and net borrowing.
These are execution rules for disbursing an already-set budget, not an instrument that changes the budget totals themselves.
Extends credit to a subset of industrial firms, a real but partial channel among many credit and industrial-policy instruments of the period.
Governs how ownership of industrial estates is transferred, a real but administrative channel shaping where industrial activity locates rather than its overall level.
A professional licensing regime for mining engineers is regulatory scaffolding with no expected effect on mine output or mineral rents.
Reorganizes ownership of a single dairy company, too narrow to move sector-wide agricultural value added or employment.
A drought-compensation mechanism cushions farmers against shocks, a real but partial force alongside weather and other farm-support programs.
A procedural directive on how to apply existing incentives, not itself a new spending instrument, so no material expected effect.
Creates a small, ongoing exit-fee revenue stream that is literally one of many minor levies making up the other-taxes bucket, but too small individually to move it.
Raising the cost of leaving the country only marginally discourages outbound travel spending.
Funds the chambers of commerce as institutions, an indirect support structure with no direct lever on business registration counts.
Organizes export clusters, a real but small coordination program relative to aggregate non-oil exports.
A procedural bylaw on budget accounting mechanics, administrative rather than a driver of any government-finance aggregate.
The direct implementing mechanism for Article 44 privatization onto the exchange, expected to visibly expand listed companies, market capitalization and traded value.
Article 44 privatization reshaped ownership of a large share of industry, a real macro force but one among several shaping GDP and industrial value added in this period.
A specific mechanism for moving preferred state shares to retail investors, a real but partial channel among several privatization routes onto the exchange.
An early privatization channel placing state shares into private hands and onto the exchange, one force among several feeding listings and market cap.
A share grant to veterans and workers is a diffuse household-wealth transfer with no expected visible effect on aggregate labor-market measures.

Nationwide consolidation of farmland into cooperatives and farm corporations directly restructured production units, a major instrument with an expected visible effect on crop output, yields and agricultural land measures.
Part of the broader White Revolution land-reform program; against economy-wide GDP and total employment it is one of many forces, not one we would expect to isolate in the line.
One of several late-1960s foreign concession agreements, a real but partial contributor to oil output and revenue alongside other contracts.
The first post-nationalization joint-venture template, but still one contract among several shaping oil production and revenue allocation.
Implements the mechanics of an existing share-transfer law, a real but administrative step in a larger privatization channel.
Export credit insurance reduces one risk factor for exporters, a real but partial instrument among many export-support tools.
A direct simplification of the tariff-band structure with a real expected effect on the applied tariff-rate series, though banding changes need not shift average levels much.
Creates rules for a new bond instrument, a narrow addition to market activity too small to move aggregate market capitalization or traded value.
Sets the core operational trading rules underpinning every exchange transaction, necessary infrastructure but not itself a driver of growth in listings, market cap or turnover.
A narrow 30-billion-rial interest-free credit line is negligible against the aggregate stock of private-sector bank credit.
A duty cut on one narrow product line, prefabricated buildings, within a much broader tariff schedule.
A minor input-cost reduction for one building material, too small to visibly move housing-unit counts.
A direct legal amendment to that year's national budget law, the exact instrument this chart's totals record.
A direct legal amendment to that year's national budget law, the exact instrument this chart's totals record.
A routine annual budget law is a small force against the overall economy, even in the context of Reza Shah-era fiscal centralization.
A routine annual budget law is a small force against the overall economy, even in the context of Reza Shah-era fiscal centralization.
A routine supplementary addition to one year's budget within the ordinary annual fiscal cycle.
Sets the post-nationalization governing framework for the insurance sector, a real structural backdrop for subsequent sector statistics though itself administrative rather than market-moving.
Extends existing duty and profit-tax exemptions for a defined set of permit holders, a real but narrow effect on tariff levels and customs revenue.
Creates a dedicated pension scheme for one narrow artisan trade, a real but small addition to the pool of Social Security pensioners.
A product-tracking enforcement tool that should push some trade into formal, taxed channels, a real but partial and hard-to-size effect.
A court ruling on whether petrochemicals are a monopoly good directly shapes pricing and output allocation for this specific sector, a real if partial effect.
A technical rule for calculating free-float shares changes how existing metrics are computed, not the underlying levels of listings or market capitalization.
As the annual budget law it directly sets that year's expense-to-GDP figure, though its effect on total GDP itself is smaller and more diffuse.
A routine wartime amendment to one year's budget, procedural within the ordinary fiscal cycle.
The explicit legal authorization for the government to set import tariffs, the direct mechanism that defines the tariff-rate and customs-duty series for decades.
Tariff-setting authority affects import costs and thus trade volumes only indirectly, one force among many shaping the trade-to-GDP ratio.
Opened a real institutional channel for private power generation, a partial force alongside the dominant state utility in expanding capacity and access.
One bilateral investment treaty among Iran's many, individually negligible against aggregate FDI inflows.
An exchange-rate insurance product for exporters is a marginal risk-management tool among many export-support instruments.
Sets that year's executive rules for bank credit extension, a direct annual lever on private-sector credit volume, though one year within a longer rolling series.
A narrow export-incentive payment program, small relative to aggregate export values.
A pricing mechanism used within the privatization drive, a real but narrow channel among several feeding new listings onto the exchange.
Workforce buyouts tied to privatization are a real but diffuse contributor to aggregate unemployment among many other labor-market forces.
The operational arm of the Article 44 privatization mandate, a real macro force on GDP but one among several restructuring forces of the period.
The direct legal mechanism moving state enterprise and bank and insurance shares onto the exchange, expected to visibly expand listings, market capitalization and traded value.
Privatized enterprises span several industrial sectors, a real but diffuse bearing on sectoral value-added trends.
Targets currency smuggling directly, a real lever on the gap between official and parallel exchange rates, though one force among many driving that rate.
Anti-smuggling enforcement only marginally shifts how much trade passes through official versus informal channels.
Special economic zone incentives are a real but diffuse program relative to aggregate national exports and FDI.
A direct regulatory lever on bank asset quality that should support private credit extension, a real but partial force among many drivers of bank lending.
The principal legal framework governing essentially all FDI into Iran, a major instrument with a material expected effect on inflow volumes, though geopolitics and sanctions also drive the series.
A named instrument explicitly aimed at curbing fuel consumption through transit expansion and consumption controls, a real but gradual and partial effect.
Mandatory building-efficiency standards are a real but partial force among the several sectors driving national energy intensity.
One article's implementing bylaw within a broad, diffuse financing-reform package, with no direct lever on industrial output growth.
Governs collection of a defined set of non-oil government fees, a real but narrow revenue stream within total tax and budget revenue.
A narrow customs-administration regime for temporary imports and exports, procedural within the broader trade system.
One article's implementing bylaw within a broad, diffuse financing-reform package, with no direct lever on industrial output growth or aggregate private credit.
Implements the legal umbrella for Iran's energy-subsidy and pricing reforms, a direct-purpose lever on energy intensity and fuel consumption, though execution-dependent and gradual.
One article's implementing bylaw within a broad, diffuse financing-reform package, with no direct lever on industrial output growth or aggregate private credit.
A direct revision of duty rates for certain imported items, a real but narrow effect on the broader tariff and import series.
A vehicle-scrappage regime creates real replacement demand, a partial but named channel into new motor vehicle production.
Retiring older vehicles is a secondary, diffuse channel into national fuel consumption.
A dedicated tariff regime for the vehicle sector, a real and sector-defining effect on vehicle-related duty rates and the protection of domestic auto assembly.
Directly nationalized the insurance and credit institutions, the structural event that reorganized ownership of the entire sector.
A headline sectoral nationalization within the broader revolutionary restructuring, a real macro force on GDP and investment but one among several such changes.
The implementing act of oil nationalization, the foundational event that determined who controlled and received the country's oil output and revenue.
Oil nationalization triggered a boycott-driven collapse and later consortium-era recovery, a structural break in the GDP series of its era.
The annual budget law directly sets that year's expense and revenue figures, though its effect on total GDP is smaller and more diffuse.
Directly sets that year's budget totals, expense and revenue, the definitional instrument for three of the four target series, diluted only by its smaller effect on GDP.
Channels a specific oil-boom windfall into development spending, a real but partial contributor to gross capital formation and oil-revenue allocation.
Reallocates a specific oil-revenue windfall across the state budget, a real but narrow piece of a much larger government-finance picture.
Expands lending powers of one specific bank, a real but partial channel into aggregate private-sector credit and industrial value added.
An administrative fix for winding up a subset of failed farm cooperatives, too narrow to move sector-wide agricultural output.
Governs the organizational form of a narrow segment of farming, too small to move aggregate agricultural value added.
A narrow land-tenure legal fix for structures on former crown lands, procedural rather than a driver of agricultural development.
A secondary, narrow bearing on housing tenure through settling ownership of structures on former crown lands.

The foundational law nationalizing forests and rangelands under state management, the direct legal regime these forest-area and depletion series track, with an expected material effect over the following decades.

Iran's major post-revolution land-and-housing statute, empowering the state to acquire and allocate urban land, a major instrument with an expected material effect on housing supply and tenure structure.
A significant housing and land policy, but its effect on aggregate GDP is small and indirect.
A drought-compensation and prevention mechanism cushions farmers against water shocks, a real but partial force among weather and other farm-support programs.
An early land-privatization and rural-credit instrument reshaping ownership of a defined stock of crown land, a real but bounded effect.
Shifts a bounded stock of state land into private hands, too narrow to visibly move the aggregate agricultural-land series.

Established the unified state electricity authority, the foundational instrument behind decades of generation, transmission and electrification expansion, with an expected material effect on access measures.
As the unified generation authority it shaped the fuel mix over time, but that mix depends more on resource endowment and investment choices, a partial and indirect effect.
The core statute of the White Revolution land reform, structurally reorganizing farm ownership nationwide, a major instrument with an expected material effect on the agriculture-production domain.
Directly targets the ownership structure underlying agricultural value added and productivity, a real effect though its net impact is historically contested and mixed with other forces.
Part of the broader White Revolution modernization program; against economy-wide GDP it is one of many forces, not one we would expect to isolate in the line.
A narrow targeted-credit scheme for livestock-fattening and fishing operations, too small to move national meat or fisheries production.
A narrow footnote-level tweak to tariff-code text, procedural within a much broader tariff regime.
A narrow article-level amendment to an implementing bylaw, procedural within a much broader tariff regime.
A named program funding expansion of these specific small crops, canola, safflower and tea, a real and direct instrument relative to the narrow size of these individual production series.
